description:
From /critical-review of specled_-csg (evidence-ledger-split), finding F1 [High, consensus of spec reviewer; calibrated]. MUST FIX BEFORE the csg epic branch merges — only finding producing user-visible wrong output today.

Base evidence (written by mix spec.check: gated, green-only) and head findings (spec.review: ungated, includes synthetic no_realized_by findings) come from asymmetric producers, so a subject that never had realized_by shows as 'introduced'/not-clean on any PR touching it. Also an internal contradiction between the findings_delta and triage_panel sections of .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, and test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs:194 pins the wrong side.

Files: lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex; lib/specled_ex/review.ex:44-49,127; .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md; findings_delta_test.exs:194.
Fix shape: reconcile the two spec sections, align delta classification to compare like-for-like producers, flip the wrong test assertion.
Full report: /Users/mz/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split/20260722-062318-consolidated.md

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, run-2 W1)
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex
- lib/specled_ex/review.ex
- .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md (reconcile the contradictory findings_delta vs triage_panel sections)
- test/specled_ex/review/** (incl. flipping the wrong-side assertion at findings_delta_test.exs:194)
Out of scope (intent): no changes to evidence store/sync modules; no widening of spec.review gating semantics (evidence never gates — ADR holds). Any file outside these globs must be disclosed in IMPLEMENTED.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Commit: 6365cba
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex, lib/specled_ex/review.ex, .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Review.FindingsDeltaTest "classifies the review-only no_realized_by synthetic finding as pre-existing even though base never recorded it", SpecLedEx.Review.FindingsDeltaTest "a detector_unavailable finding with a real verifier reason still classifies normally"
Verification stubs flipped: none (existing findings_delta verification target already execute: true)
Verification tier: change-scoped — test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review/html_layout_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs (175 tests, 0 failures); mix spec.check --no-run-commands (pass); mix format --check-formatted on touched files; full-system proof deferred to the merge gate / PR CI
Final-tier runs: 1

Summary: mix spec.check never computes the review-only no_realized_by detector_unavailable finding, so base evidence recorded by spec.check can never contain it; FindingsDelta.classify/3 was diffing it against base anyway, so any subject that has ever lacked realized_by read as "introduced"/not-clean on every PR touching it. Fixed FindingsDelta.classify/3 to split head findings into producer-comparable and producer-incomparable sets, folding the latter (currently just the no_realized_by reason) unconditionally into pre_existing rather than diffing it against a base that structurally can never attest to it — a genuinely new, comparable finding alongside it still classifies as introduced normally. Reconciled the findings_delta, triage_panel, and no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code spec sections around this producer-comparability rule and added a scenario pinning it, and flipped the findings_delta_test.exs integration assertion that pinned the old (wrong) behavior to assert the corrected pre-existing/clean outcome.

title: review-F1: findings delta misclassifies pre-existing findings as introduced
